channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: On-Device Function Calling in Google AI Edge Gallery
item.description: Google has launched FunctionGemma, a 270M parameter model that enables efficient on-device function calling for tasks like calendar management and hardware control, all offline. This innovation enhances mobile AI experiences by providing high-speed, low-latency performance.

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now supports various third-party integrations, allowing developers to connect their AI agents with tools like GitHub and Notion. This ecosystem aims to enhance the capabilities of AI applications in real-world scenarios.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's potential. This annual tradition aims to engage attendees before the main event.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem solving, featuring keynotes available online. The event emphasizes the transition to agentic AI and offers specialized technical tracks.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, offering faster GPU and NPU acceleration and better support for PyTorch and JAX. This update aims to enhance efficiency and security in deploying GenAI and on-device inference.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Wednesday Build Hour is a new weekly session for developers to enhance their cloud skills through hands-on building. Led by Google Cloud experts, it covers advanced topics like AI agents and productivity tools.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has released updates to streamline coding workflows, introducing tools like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These features aim to make AI a more tailored collaborator for developers.

item.title: Microsoft Teams: Sharing recap access
item.description: Microsoft Teams has introduced a feature allowing recording and transcript owners to grant access to specific individuals without requests. This aims to simplify sharing and enhance collaboration.

item.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
item.description: OpenAI has shuffled its executive team, appointing COO Brad Lightcap to lead special projects, while CMO Kate Rouch steps away for health reasons. This change reflects ongoing leadership dynamics at OpenAI.

item.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
item.description: OpenClaw, an AI agentic tool, has been reported to allow attackers to gain unauthorized admin access silently. This raises significant security concerns regarding AI tools.

item.title: Anthropic buys biotech startup Coefficient Bio in $400M deal: Reports
item.description: Anthropic has acquired biotech startup Coefficient Bio for $400 million, expanding its capabilities in AI and biotech integration. This move highlights the growing intersection of AI and life sciences.

item.title: Anthropic ramps up its political activities with a new PAC
item.description: Anthropic has launched a new political action committee (PAC) to support candidates aligned with its AI policy goals ahead of the midterm elections. This reflects the company's increasing involvement in shaping AI regulations.

item.title: AI companies are building huge natural gas plants to power data centers. What could go wrong?
item.description: Major tech companies like Meta, Microsoft, and Google are investing in natural gas plants to power their AI data centers, raising concerns about environmental impacts and sustainability. Critics warn this could lead to long-term regrets.

item.title: People would rather have an Amazon warehouse in their backyard than a data center
item.description: A recent poll reveals that people prefer having an Amazon warehouse over a data center in their neighborhoods, indicating ongoing community concerns about data center impacts. This reflects the complex public sentiment surrounding tech infrastructure.

item.title: OpenAI’s AGI boss is taking a leave of absence
item.description: OpenAI's CEO of AGI deployment, Fidji Simo, is taking a medical leave of absence, leading to further changes in the company's leadership. This adds to the ongoing shifts within OpenAI's executive team.

item.title: The Facebook insider building content moderation for the AI era
item.description: Moonbounce has secured $12 million to enhance its AI content moderation system, which aims to translate moderation policies into consistent AI actions. This development is crucial for improving online content management.

item.title: Apple’s best product ever
item.description: In celebration of its 50th anniversary, Apple has engaged its audience to rank its top 50 products, receiving over 1.6 million votes. This reflects the company's significant impact on technology and consumer culture.

item.title: Chatbots are now prescribing psychiatric drugs
item.description: Utah has permitted an AI system to prescribe psychiatric medications without a doctor, marking a significant step in AI's role in healthcare. While it could reduce costs, concerns about safety and transparency persist.

item.title: claude-monitor-usage 2.0.4
item.description: A new update for Claude Pro/Max introduces a real-time usage monitor with features like notifications and mini mode, enhancing user experience. This reflects ongoing improvements in AI tools for better user engagement.

item.title: claude-swap 0.7.0b4
item.description: The latest update for Claude Code includes a multi-account switcher, simplifying user management across different accounts. This is part of ongoing enhancements to improve usability.

item.title: Bench mark of 'Claude in Excel'
item.description: A Mathematica user suggests strategic policy changes to improve the software's adoption, highlighting its core issues. This recommendation aims to enhance user experience and accessibility.

item.title: pylumbergh 0.1.0a131
item.description: Pylumbergh 0.1.0a131 is released, providing a self-hosted web dashboard for managing multiple Claude Code AI sessions in tmux. This tool enhances user control over AI operations.

item.title: pylumbergh 0.13.3
item.description: Pylumbergh 0.13.3 is another update for the self-hosted web dashboard for Claude Code AI sessions. It continues to support efficient management of AI tasks.

item.title: mamba-scan-lite added to PyPI
item.description: Mamba-scan-lite has been added to PyPI, offering a memory-efficient scanning tool for HuggingFace Transformers. This update addresses out-of-memory issues on smaller GPUs.

item.title: reeln-plugin-openai 0.8.2
item.description: The reeln-plugin-openai 0.8.2 has been released, integrating OpenAI-powered LLM capabilities into the reeln-cli. This plugin supports metadata handling and translation.

item.title: 73% of B2B Buyers Use AI Tools in Purchase Research, Multi-Source Analysis Finds
item.description: A study reveals that 73% of B2B buyers utilize AI tools for purchase research, with AI search outperforming Google organic results. However, many marketers lack AI strategies.

item.title: Unraveling Solo Ball's decline: How under-the-radar wrist injury has led to UConn guard's shooting woes
item.description: UConn guard Solo Ball discusses how a wrist injury has affected his performance and shooting ability. This insight highlights the challenges athletes face with injuries.

item.title: cupel 0.1.55
item.description: Cupel 0.1.55 is released, allowing separation of precious LLMs from base LLMs, compatible with OpenAI and Anthropic APIs. This enhances flexibility in AI model usage.

item.title: Microsoft Teams: Annotations on Teams Single Window Sharing on macOS
item.description: Microsoft Teams introduces a feature allowing presenters to share a single application window with annotation capabilities. This aims to enhance presentation quality and privacy.

item.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Loss Prevention – Block External Domain/User action in DLP for SharePoint/OneDrive
item.description: Microsoft Purview enhances Data Loss Prevention by allowing admins to block external domain access in SharePoint and OneDrive. This strengthens data security and compliance.

item.title: Microsoft Purview: Purview in Microsoft Admin Center
item.description: Microsoft has integrated Purview into the Admin Center, allowing IT admins to manage oversharing risks and ensure data protection for M365 Copilot. This feature aims to enhance the secure adoption of AI tools within organizations.

item.title: Microsoft Viva: Viva Glint and Viva Insights Integration - Exclusion List for data sharing
item.description: Microsoft Viva Glint now allows admins to create an exclusion list for data sharing in Viva Insights, promoting privacy and consistent data management. This feature will be available in May 2026.

item.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Loss Prevention – Inline DLP for Prompts for Microsoft Foundry apps and agents
item.description: Purview will enable inline data loss prevention (DLP) policies for prompts in Microsoft Foundry apps, ensuring the protection of sensitive information. This feature is set to be available in June 2026.

item.title: OpenAI acquires TBPN, the buzzy founder-led business talk show - TechCrunch
item.description: OpenAI has acquired TBPN, a popular business talk show focused on the tech industry, aiming to reshape the narrative around AI. This acquisition highlights OpenAI's expansion into media.

item.title: Samsung may skip upgrading a key Galaxy Z Fold 8 and Z Flip 8 component for a third year in a row
item.description: Samsung is reportedly skipping a significant upgrade for its Galaxy Z Fold 8 and Z Flip 8 models for the third consecutive year, likely due to cost-cutting measures.

item.title: agentpay-langchain 0.1.0
item.description: The release of agentpay-langchain 0.1.0 introduces a tool for making autonomous USDC payments via AgentPay, enhancing payment automation capabilities.

item.title: agentpay-langchain added to PyPI
item.description: Agentpay-langchain has been added to PyPI, allowing developers to access the tool for autonomous USDC payments through AgentPay.

item.title: miao-io 0.1.0
item.description: Miao-io 0.1.0 has been released, offering scalable PyTorch data loaders for OME-NGFF zarr datasets, enhancing data handling capabilities in machine learning.

item.title: miao-io added to PyPI
item.description: Miao-io has been added to PyPI, providing scalable data loaders for OME-NGFF zarr datasets, which support efficient data management in PyTorch.

item.title: TBPN makes millions by covering Silicon Valley like ‘SportsCenter’. OpenAI just scooped it up.
item.description: TBPN has gained significant revenue by covering Silicon Valley like a sports league, and OpenAI's acquisition aims to leverage this unique approach to engage with tech narratives.

item.title: Sources detail Fidji Simo's moves at OpenAI, including spearheading the TBPN acquisition and pushing OpenAI to cut Sora and avoid other social media products (Stephanie Palazzolo/The Information)
item.description: Fidji Simo has taken significant steps at OpenAI, including leading the acquisition of TBPN and advocating for the discontinuation of the Sora project and other social media initiatives. These moves indicate a strategic shift in OpenAI's focus under Simo's leadership.

item.title: OpenAI: Masters of Agitprop 2.0
item.description: The article explores the role of media in shaping public perception and political movements, drawing parallels between historical propaganda and modern AI-driven narratives. It critiques how AI can be used to manipulate information.

item.title: The Blatant Reasons for the Destruction of NSF (and other federal science agencies)
item.description: The article discusses the detrimental impacts on the National Science Foundation and other federal science agencies, arguing that their destruction undermines scientific progress. It highlights the importance of these institutions in fostering innovation.

item.title: Nobody Could Answer the CFO’s Question on the ROI from AI Adoption; TechWish Built a Platform That Can
item.description: TechWish has developed a platform that connects AI usage patterns to measurable business outcomes, addressing a common concern among CFOs regarding the return on investment from AI adoption. This solution aims to bridge the gap between AI deployment and business value.

item.title: llama-index-truthvouch added to PyPI
item.description: The LlamaIndex integration for TruthVouch Trust API has been added to PyPI, focusing on hallucination detection and content verification. This tool aims to enhance the reliability of AI-generated content.

item.title: llama-index-truthvouch 1.0.0
item.description: Version 1.0.0 of the LlamaIndex integration for TruthVouch Trust API has been released, emphasizing features for hallucination detection and content verification. This update aims to bolster the accuracy of AI-generated information.

item.title: langchain-truthvouch 1.0.0
item.description: LangChain's integration for the TruthVouch Trust API has reached version 1.0.0, focusing on hallucination detection and content verification. This integration aims to improve the quality of AI-generated content.

item.title: langchain-truthvouch added to PyPI
item.description: The LangChain integration for TruthVouch Trust API has been added to PyPI, providing tools for hallucination detection and content verification. This is part of ongoing efforts to ensure AI-generated content is trustworthy.

item.title: langchain-core 1.2.25
item.description: LangChain has released version 1.2.25, which focuses on building applications with large language models through composability. This update aims to enhance the flexibility and usability of LangChain for developers.

item.title: Sony's gaming division just bought an AI startup that turns photos into 3D volumes
item.description: Sony Interactive Entertainment has acquired Cinemersive Labs, a UK startup that specializes in transforming 2D photos and videos into 3D volumes. This acquisition aims to enhance Sony's capabilities in visual computing.
